steps.title: Use two points from the graph
steps.content: Do this as a Desmos table instead of estimating by hand. The graphed line clearly passes through $(-2,-3)$ and $(0,9)$. Enter those two points, then run a linear regression. Desmos gives $m=6$ and $b=9$, so the graphed line is $y=6x+9$.

steps.title: Build the equation
steps.content: Rewrite the regression equation so it matches the answer choices. From $y=6x+9$, multiply by 2 to make the constant 18: $$2y=12x+18$$ Move the $12x$ term to the left: $$-12x+2y=18$$ This has the form $-12x+Ry=18$ with $R=2$.

steps.title: Match the positive constant
steps.content: Since $R=2$ is positive, $-12x+Ry=18$ is allowed. That is choice D, so D is correct.
